description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a camouflage paint scheme in green, brown, and black, designed for urban or tactical operations. The fuselage is streamlined with a rounded nose, a large cockpit area, and a slightly raised tail boom. The helicopter features a four-blade main rotor and a two-blade tail rotor, both in a dark gray color. The landing gear consists of two robust wheels at the front and a single wheel at the rear, providing stability on various surfaces. The cockpit windows are large, multi-paneled, and slightly tinted, offering wide visibility for the pilots. The overall build suggests a metal and composite construction, typical of modern utility helicopters.
description: A camouflaged military helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, large tinted cockpit windows, and wheeled landing gear. Its green, brown, and black paint scheme and robust build are suited for urban or tactical environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, large cockpit windows, and the full span of the four-blade main rotor.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the camouflage pattern, cockpit profile, landing wheels, and the tail rotor configuration.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the tapering fuselage with visible landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the main rotor, cockpit, and the continuation of the camouflage paint scheme.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four main rotor blades, long and straight with slightly tapered tips, made of composite material, colored dark gray with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored gray, with no visible protective shroud or markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Not present; instead, the helicopter uses a tricycle-style wheeled landing gear with two main wheels at the front and a single wheel at the rear, all in dark gray.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five cockpit windows: two large front panels, two angled side panels, and one overhead panel, all slightly tinted with black framing and no visible markings.
